Method.doc:
Initialize the WindowManager spoon

Parameters:
 * None

Returns:
 * The WindowManager object
Method.name: init

Method.desc: Configure the WindowManager with profiles and layouts
Method.doc:
Configure the WindowManager with profiles and layouts

Parameters:
 * config - A table containing configuration:
   * debug - boolean, enable debug logging (default: false)
   * notifications - boolean, enable notifications (default: true)
   * profiles - table of screen name to profile mappings, where each profile contains:
     * tiles - table of tile definitions, each tile having:
       * anchor - string, position anchor like "topleft", "center", "bottomright" etc.
       * w, h - numbers, absolute width and height in pixels
       * OR wPct, hPct - numbers, percentage of screen (0.0 to 1.0)
     * layouts - array of layout definitions, each containing:
       * name - string, layout name
       * hotkey - table with 'mods' array and 'key' string for hotkey binding
       * space_layouts - table with numeric indices representing spaces:
         * [1] - table of tileName to bundle ID(s) mappings for tiling
         * [2+] - arrays of bundle IDs for fullscreen spaces
       * focusApp - optional string, bundle ID of app to focus after layout

Returns:
 * The WindowManager object

Notes:
 * Screen names must match exactly as reported by macOS (e.g., "Built-in Retina Display")
 * Bundle identifiers can be found using: osascript -e 'id of app "AppName"'
 * Space 1 is always used for tiling, spaces 2+ for fullscreen apps
Method.name: configure

Method.doc:
Start the WindowManager

Parameters:
 * None

Returns:
 * The WindowManager object

Notes:
 * Must be called after configure() to begin monitoring screens and binding hotkeys
 * Automatically detects screen changes and switches profiles
 * Enables space caching for improved performance
Method.name: start

Method.doc:
Stop the WindowManager

Parameters:
 * None

Returns:
 * The WindowManager object

Notes:
 * Stops all screen and space monitoring
 * Unbinds all hotkeys
 * Cleans up any active operations
Method.name: stop

Method.doc:
Bind hotkeys for WindowManager

Parameters:
 * mapping - A table with hotkey mappings (currently unused)

Returns:
 * The WindowManager object

Notes:
 * This method exists for API consistency
 * Hotkeys are actually defined in the layout configuration within each profile
 * Each layout can have its own hotkey binding
Method.name: bindHotkeys
parameters: * mapping - A table with hotkey mappings

Method.doc:
Apply a specific layout

Parameters:
 * layout - A layout table with space_layouts specification:
   * name - string, layout name (optional)
   * space_layouts - table with numeric indices:
     * [1] - tile mappings
     * [2+] - fullscreen app lists
   * focusApp - optional string, bundle ID to focus

Returns:
 * The WindowManager object

Notes:
 * Can be called programmatically to apply a layout without using hotkeys
 * Will launch apps if they're not running
 * Cancels any currently running layout application
 * Normalizes existing fullscreen spaces before applying new layout
Method.name: apply_layout

Method.desc: Get the current status of the WindowManager
Method.doc:
Get the current status of the WindowManager

Parameters:
 * None

Returns:
 * A string describing the current status including:
   * Current screen name
   * Whether a layout is being applied
   * Number of active hotkeys
   * Screen and space watcher status
   * Number of active operations
   * Space cache details
Method.name: status
returns: A string describing the current status

desc: Advanced window manager with tiling and fullscreen management
